spec_match.rationale: Three animations with different durations / iteration counts / one in reverse, averaged into a parent gauge — uses overallProgress as the canonical normalisation point, which is the spec's stated rationale.

interactive.rationale: Play / pause / reset controls drive three concurrent animations and surface live per-animation gauges plus an averaged total.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a parent 'average progress' bar across three differently-shaped animations; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't handle one animation finishing before the others (the averaged total continues averaging in a finished animation as 1, which is correct but unsurfaced); no scrub/seek; no demonstration of what happens if one animation is cancelled mid-flight (its overallProgress goes null).

references.rationale: see-also section links Iteration meter / Scroll vs Time / Feature index / spec, but is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5083257285378048 link required by CLAUDE.md invariant 3.

design_system.rationale: All colours via --accent-blue / --accent-emerald / --accent-rose etc. Buttons inherit global styling; box overrides background + text colour together correctly.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The see-also list is missing https://chromestatus.com/feature/5083257285378048. CLAUDE.md invariant 3 requires every concept page to embed the chromestatus link for the slug-recovery script to work. Add it as a list item.

openQuestions.title: Show a cancelled / null-progress animation
openQuestions.detail: If one of the three animations is cancelled, its overallProgress becomes null per spec, and the average should account for that (currently the code treats it as 0 by hiding the support banner). Add a 'cancel B' button and surface how the dashboard handles null — that's the realistic orchestration case.

openQuestions.title: Annotate that finished animations stay at 1.0
openQuestions.detail: When animation A (1x) finishes after 1.2s, the total continues averaging A=1 + B + C. That's expected, but the dashboard doesn't make it visible — a 'done' badge next to each gauge would reinforce that overallProgress saturates rather than re-cycling.

summary: Strong real-world orchestration story showing why one normalised progress per animation matters. Two gaps: missing chromestatus reference link (a hard rule) and no surfacing of the null/cancel case that the spec explicitly covers.
